# QuickTaxReturn — CPA Intake Package Template
# Generated by QuickTaxReturn for Tax Year 2025
# This document is a structured handoff from QuickTaxReturn to a licensed CPA.
# AGENT INSTRUCTION: Populate every section with data collected during the session.
# Mark any field as [NOT PROVIDED] if the user did not supply it.
# Mark any field as [NOT APPLICABLE] if it does not apply to the taxpayer.

---

## HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE

When a user is being handed off to a CPA, populate this template with all data collected during the QuickTaxReturn session and present the completed document to the user. Instruct the user:

> "Here's your intake package. Bring this to your CPA appointment — it has everything organized so they can get started quickly. This can reduce your billable time."

Present as plain text or formatted document. Do not include raw document images (privacy).

---

## INTAKE PACKAGE

**Prepared by:** QuickTaxReturn (AI-assisted tax preparation)
**Date prepared:** [DATE]
**Tax year:** 2025 (return due April 15, 2026)
**Reason for CPA referral:** [STATE THE SPECIFIC TRIGGER — e.g., "Taxpayer has self-employment income (1099-NEC) requiring Schedule C" or "Multi-state filing situation"]

---

## SECTION 1 — TAXPAYER INFORMATION

```
Legal name (primary):          [FIRST] [LAST]
Social Security Number:        [XXX-XX-XXXX] — confirm with original card
Date of birth:                 [MM/DD/YYYY]
Filing status:                 [ ] Single  [ ] MFJ  [ ] MFS  [ ] HOH  [ ] QSS

Spouse legal name (if MFJ/MFS): [FIRST] [LAST]
Spouse SSN:                    [XXX-XX-XXXX]
Spouse date of birth:          [MM/DD/YYYY]

Mailing address:               [STREET]
                               [CITY, STATE, ZIP]
Phone:                         [PHONE]
Email:                         [EMAIL]

Occupation (taxpayer):         [JOB TITLE / INDUSTRY]
Occupation (spouse, if MFJ):   [JOB TITLE / INDUSTRY]

Prior year AGI (used for e-file identity verification):  [AMOUNT or NOT PROVIDED]
Prior year return filed:       [ ] Yes — by whom: [SELF / CPA NAME]  [ ] No  [ ] Unknown
```

---

## SECTION 3 — INCOME DOCUMENTS

### W-2 Wages (one block per W-2)

#### W-2 #1
```
Employer name:                 [NAME]
Employer EIN:                  [XX-XXXXXXX]
W-2 Box 1  — Wages:            $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 2  — Fed tax withheld: $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 3  — SS wages:         $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 4  — SS tax withheld:  $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 5  — Medicare wages:   $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 6  — Medicare withheld:$[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 12 — Code(s):          [CODE]: $[AMOUNT]  [CODE]: $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 13 — Retirement plan:  [ ] Yes  [ ] No
W-2 Box 14 — Other:            [DESCRIPTION]: $[AMOUNT]
State (Box 15):                [STATE]
State wages (Box 16):          $[AMOUNT]
State tax withheld (Box 17):   $[AMOUNT]
```

```
Employer name:                 [NAME]
Employer EIN:                  [XX-XXXXXXX]
W-2 Box 1  — Wages:            $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 2  — Fed tax withheld: $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 3  — SS wages:         $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 4  — SS tax withheld:  $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 5  — Medicare wages:   $[AMOUNT]
W-2 Box 6  — Medicare withheld:$[AMOUNT]

State wages (Box 16):          $[AMOUNT]
State tax withheld (Box 17):   $[AMOUNT]
```

### Interest Income (1099-INT) — one block per payer

#### 1099-INT #1
```
Payer name:                    [BANK / INSTITUTION NAME]
Box 1 — Interest income:       $[AMOUNT]
Box 4 — Federal tax withheld:  $[AMOUNT]
Box 8 — Tax-exempt interest:   $[AMOUNT]
Box 11 — Bond premium:         $[AMOUNT]
```

*(Add additional 1099-INT blocks as needed)*

---

### Dividend Income (1099-DIV) — one block per payer

#### 1099-DIV #1
```
Payer name:                    [BROKERAGE / FUND NAME]
Box 1a — Total ordinary divs:  $[AMOUNT]
Box 1b — Qualified dividends:  $[AMOUNT]
Box 2a — Total cap gain dist:  $[AMOUNT]
Box 4  — Federal tax withheld: $[AMOUNT]
Box 11 — REIT dividends (Sec 199A): $[AMOUNT]
```

*(Add additional 1099-DIV blocks as needed)*

---

### Retirement Distributions (1099-R) — one block per distribution

#### 1099-R #1
```
Payer name:                    [INSTITUTION NAME]
Account type:                  [ ] IRA  [ ] 401(k)  [ ] Pension  [ ] 403(b)  [ ] Other: [TYPE]
Box 1  — Gross distribution:   $[AMOUNT]
Box 2a — Taxable amount:       $[AMOUNT]
Box 2b — Taxable unknown:      [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Box 4  — Federal tax withheld: $[AMOUNT]
Box 7  — Distribution code:    [CODE]
Box 7  — IRA/SEP/SIMPLE:       [ ] Yes  [ ] No
State tax withheld (Box 14):   $[AMOUNT]
Notes:                         [e.g., "Rollover completed within 60 days", "RMD", etc.]
```

*(Add additional 1099-R blocks as needed)*

---

### Social Security Benefits (SSA-1099)
```
Received SSA-1099:             [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Box 5 — Net benefits received: $[AMOUNT]
Box 6 — Voluntary federal withholding: $[AMOUNT]
Medicare premiums deducted:    $[AMOUNT]
```

---

### Unemployment Compensation (1099-G)
```
Received 1099-G:               [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Payer (state agency):          [STATE NAME]
Box 1 — Unemployment comp:     $[AMOUNT]
Box 2 — State/local refund:    $[AMOUNT]
Box 4 — Federal tax withheld:  $[AMOUNT]
Prior year itemized deductions: [ ] Yes  [ ] No  [ ] Unknown
(Box 2 is only taxable if taxpayer itemized in the prior year)
```

---

### OUT-OF-SCOPE INCOME — Documents Received (CPA Must Handle)

List any documents received that triggered the CPA referral. Do not attempt to enter values — provide document list only.

```
[ ] 1099-NEC received from:    [PAYER NAME(S)]
    Approximate amount:        $[AMOUNT]
    Business activity:         [DESCRIPTION — e.g., "freelance graphic design"]

[ ] 1099-B received from:      [BROKERAGE NAME(S)]
    Number of transactions:    [NUMBER or UNKNOWN]
    Assets sold:               [e.g., "stocks, mutual funds, ETFs"]

[ ] 1099-K received from:      [PLATFORM NAME — e.g., "Venmo, PayPal, Etsy"]
    Box 1a — Gross amount:     $[AMOUNT]

[ ] 1099-MISC received from:   [PAYER NAME]
    Box type and amount:       [e.g., "Box 2 royalties: $X"]

[ ] 1099-DA received from:     [EXCHANGE NAME]
    (Digital assets / crypto)

[ ] 1099-C received from:      [LENDER NAME]
    Box 2 — Cancelled debt:    $[AMOUNT]

[ ] 1099-S received from:      [SETTLEMENT AGENT / TITLE CO]
    Box 2 — Gross proceeds:    $[AMOUNT]

[ ] K-1 received from:         [ENTITY NAME]
    Entity type:               [ ] Partnership  [ ] S-corp  [ ] Trust  [ ] Estate

[ ] 1098 (mortgage interest) from: [LENDER NAME]
    Box 1 — Mortgage interest: $[AMOUNT]
    Box 5 — Mortgage insurance premium: $[AMOUNT]
    Box 10 — Property taxes:   $[AMOUNT]

[ ] Other documents:           [DESCRIBE]
```

## SECTION 4 — ABOVE-THE-LINE DEDUCTIONS (ADJUSTMENTS TO INCOME)

### Student Loan Interest (1098-E)
```
Received 1098-E:               [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Lender name:                   [NAME]
Box 1 — Interest paid:         $[AMOUNT]
Loan used for:                 [ ] Taxpayer  [ ] Spouse  [ ] Dependent
```

### IRA Contributions
```
Traditional IRA contributed:   [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Amount contributed for TY2025: $[AMOUNT]
  (Contribution deadline: April 15, 2026)
Roth IRA contributed:          [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Amount:                        $[AMOUNT]
Covered by workplace plan:     [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Spouse covered by workplace plan: [ ] Yes  [ ] No  [ ] N/A
```

### Health Savings Account (HSA) — Form 8889
```
HSA contributions (self, not payroll): $[AMOUNT]
HSA contributions (employer):          $[AMOUNT]
HSA distributions taken:              $[AMOUNT]
Coverage type during 2025:            [ ] Self-only  [ ] Family  [ ] None
```

### Other Adjustments (flag for CPA — do not calculate)
```
[ ] Self-employed health insurance premiums
[ ] Self-employed SEP/SIMPLE/qualified plan contributions
[ ] Alimony paid (pre-2019 divorce agreements only)
[ ] Educator expenses (note: QuickTaxReturn in scope — $300 max for K-12 educators)
    Educator expenses paid: $[AMOUNT]
[ ] Moving expenses (military only)
[ ] Other: [DESCRIBE]
```

---

## SECTION 5 — CREDITS

### Child Tax Credit / Additional Child Tax Credit
*(Populated from dependent information in Section 2)*
```
Qualifying children under 17:  [NUMBER]
Children who have SSNs:        [NUMBER]
```

### Child and Dependent Care Expenses (Form 2441)
```
Paid for dependent care:       [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Provider name:                 [NAME]
Provider tax ID (EIN or SSN):  [NUMBER]
Amount paid:                   $[AMOUNT]
Employer-provided care benefits (W-2 Box 10): $[AMOUNT]
Both spouses worked/sought work: [ ] Yes  [ ] No  [ ] N/A
```

### Education Credits (Form 8863)
```
Student name:                  [NAME]
School name:                   [INSTITUTION]
1098-T Box 1 — Payments:       $[AMOUNT]
1098-T Box 5 — Scholarships:   $[AMOUNT]
Year of study (1–4 / beyond):  [YEAR]
First 4 years complete:        [ ] Yes (→ LLC only)  [ ] No (→ AOTC eligible)
Felony drug conviction:        [ ] Yes  [ ] No
```

### Earned Income Tax Credit
*(Determined from income data and dependent counts above)*
```
Self-reported earned income:   $[AMOUNT]
Investment income:             $[AMOUNT]
Valid SSNs for all:            [ ] Yes  [ ] No
```

### Energy Credits (Form 5695)
```
Home energy improvements made: [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Type:                          [e.g., solar panels, heat pump, insulation, EV charger]
Amount paid:                   $[AMOUNT]
Renter or owner:               [ ] Owner  [ ] Renter
```

### Other Credits (flag for CPA)
```
[ ] Adoption credit (Form 8839)
[ ] Foreign tax credit (Form 1116)
[ ] Residential clean energy credit (complex situation)
[ ] Premium Tax Credit (Form 8962) — had ACA marketplace coverage
    1095-A received: [ ] Yes  [ ] No
    SLCSP column B amount: $[AMOUNT]
```

## SECTION 7 — PRIOR YEAR & SPECIAL SITUATIONS

```

Received IRS correspondence in 2025: [ ] Yes — describe: [DESC]  [ ] No
Made estimated tax payments (Form 1040-ES): [ ] Yes — total paid: $[AMOUNT]  [ ] No
Had health insurance all year: [ ] Yes  [ ] No — months without: [NUMBER]
ACA marketplace coverage:      [ ] Yes (need Form 1095-A)  [ ] No
Applied for FAFSA:             [ ] Yes  [ ] No
Received an IP PIN from IRS:   [ ] Yes — PIN: [6-DIGIT PIN]  [ ] No
```

## SECTION 8 — QUICKTAXRETURN PRELIMINARY CALCULATIONS

*Complete this section only if QuickTaxReturn completed a full calculation before escalating.*
*If escalation happened early in the session, mark all fields [NOT CALCULATED].*

```
Filing status used:            [STATUS]
Total income (gross):          $[AMOUNT]
Adjustments to income:        −$[AMOUNT]
Adjusted Gross Income (AGI):   $[AMOUNT]
Deduction method:              [ ] Standard ($[AMOUNT])  [ ] Itemizing (refer to CPA)
Taxable income:                $[AMOUNT]
Tentative income tax:          $[AMOUNT]
Child Tax Credit:             −$[AMOUNT]
EITC:                         −$[AMOUNT]
Other credits:                −$[AMOUNT]
Total tax liability:           $[AMOUNT]
Total withholding:            −$[AMOUNT]
Estimated refund / balance due: [REFUND: $AMOUNT  or  BALANCE DUE: $AMOUNT]

Note: These are QuickTaxReturn's preliminary figures. The CPA should independently verify
all values using the source documents listed in Sections 3–6.
```

---

## SECTION 9 — DOCUMENTS CHECKLIST

Use this checklist to confirm what was provided and what may still be needed.

```
[ ] W-2(s) — all employers
[ ] 1099-INT — all banks and financial institutions
[ ] 1099-DIV — all brokerages
[ ] 1099-R — all retirement distributions
[ ] SSA-1099 — Social Security benefits
[ ] 1099-G — unemployment / state refund
[ ] 1098-E — student loan interest
[ ] 1098-T — tuition (if education credit claimed)
[ ] 1095-A — ACA marketplace (if applicable)
[ ] 1098 — mortgage interest (if itemizing)
[ ] 1099-NEC — self-employment (CPA territory)
[ ] 1099-B — investment sales (CPA territory)
[ ] 1099-K — payment processors (CPA territory)
[ ] K-1 — partnership / S-corp / trust (CPA territory)
[ ] Prior year federal return (Form 1040)
[ ] Prior year state return
[ ] IP PIN (if assigned by IRS)
[ ] Photo ID (required by some CPA firms for e-file authorization)
```

Missing documents noted by taxpayer:
```
[LIST ANY DOCUMENTS THE TAXPAYER MENTIONED THEY HAVEN'T RECEIVED YET]
```

---

## SECTION 10 — TAXPAYER NOTES AND QUESTIONS

*Record any questions or concerns the taxpayer raised during the QuickTaxReturn session.*

```
1. [QUESTION OR CONCERN]
2. [QUESTION OR CONCERN]
3. [QUESTION OR CONCERN]
```

Taxpayer's primary concern:
```
[ ] Maximize refund
[ ] Minimize tax owed
[ ] Avoid penalties
[ ] File correctly / avoid audit risk
[ ] Understand their tax situation better
[ ] Planning for next year
[ ] Other: [DESCRIBE]
```

*This intake package was generated by QuickTaxReturn on [DATE]. QuickTaxReturn is an AI-assisted tax preparation tool, not a licensed tax advisor. All values should be independently verified by the receiving CPA using original source documents. The taxpayer is responsible for the accuracy of their return.*
